review_text: Timeless classic - absolutely astounding.

An amazingly directed and groundbreaking samurai film that revolutionized modern action & adventure movies. It's such a famous story that has influenced so many other movies and TV shows. Akira Kurosawa's excellent direction and pacing makes this such a breeze to watch. The acting and physicality of the cast is so emotional and expressive. The movie has excellent cinematography for the time. Despite the tension, this movie is also very funny. The titular seven samurai are all very likable. My favorite is the hilarious wild card Kikuchiyo. There's also a sad subplot that subverts the 'star-crossed lovers" romance trope. There are several heartbreaking moments, one where the farmer finds his kidnapped wife, and two emotional breakdowns that reveal Kikuchyo's tragic backstory. The final showdown against the bandits is very exciting and well-choreographed. The ending is very bittersweet, as the village is saved, but at the cost of 4 of the samurai and the uncertainty of their future.
Possibly the best movie of all time.
a long movie but refreshing..

The Greatest Movie Ever Made. The influence of this film can be felt in almost every cinematic experience since its debut. Inspired the excellent Magnifincent Seven. Akira Kurasowa proves the best effects are practical. Both in scope and presenting the human perspective. Live,Love, Fight.

The final thought of the samurai leader will stick with you until the credits start rolling: "The villagers have one, not us." The importance of community through thick and thin is the biggest take away. Seeing the journey of the seven samurai to accomplish one goal helps you to remember how hard one's journey can be. The victory may not be what you were expecting.

If you are wondering if there is a good reason for this movie to be more than 3 hours long, let me tell you: YES. There is almost no wasted time. It is a great movie, very interesting plot and the characters are great. Fantastic! even if you are not a fan of samurai stories.

Widely regarded as one of the greatest and most influential movies ever made, Seven Samurai is not only a testament to the brilliance of the late great Akira Kurosawa, but a massive showcase in enthralling storytelling, terrific action, structural mastery and faceted characterizations.
Unmistakable classic

The epitome of cinema. This classic epic has everything; suspense, dispair, humour, remorse, hope, triumph, and enough action scenes to keep the deep running plot moving. Not an easy accomplishment for a nearly 4 hour movie, even more impressive to have done it so expertly.

They don’t do good but for Japan for 10 to 15,000,000 budget movie Godzilla minus one managed to bomb in the box office in a good way it just shows that Japan isn’t Messing around The effect on these Japanese movies really shows that Japan has been getting higher and higher in the marketing campaign them better than America back to seven samurai with great special-effects. great story, emotional scenes amazing character all in one movie, not even a Trilogy All the stuff I just named is just in one movie either way it’s a great watch. Definitely recommended.

The greatest Japanese film in history! Today I concluded my viewing of some of the greatest works of Japanese cinema, and I concluded by re-watching this masterpiece by Akira Kurosawa, which I consider to be the greatest Japanese work, the greatest samurai film in history, and the best Kurosawa film. I watched this film last year and it was a wonderful experience, but after re-watching and enjoying the film, I felt its greatness and beauty, and I must have I pay tribute to the role of the legend Toshiro Mifune as Kikuchio, in which he embodied a fun but sad character and gave us the most beautiful sacrifices at the end of the film. In fact, all the acting crew excelled in their roles and each character had its own aesthetics. The film was a mixture of war, battles, poverty, wisdom, planning, romance, courage and comedy, which Toshiro Mifune created. In general, the movie was legendary and one of the best movies in history, and the closing scene was perfect and what a great masterpiece
